---
title: "How can I use the BioHUG resource as a HUG or University of Geneva researcher ?"
editor: visual
---

More details on how researchers can use BioHUG data will be available here in the summer 2026. Below is some early information:

BioHUG was set up in 2024 and started banking DNA samples from patients who have signed the general consent in February 2025. It was approved by the HUG Directorate and supported by the Faculty of Medicine. The HUG informatics team set up a system that produces daily lists of blood samples that come from patients who have signed the general consent. These lists are processed 2 or 3 times a week to ensure no duplication. A laboratory technician from Medical Genetics takes the list and picks up spare blood samples from haematology in batches of 48. The technician checks the samples are not clotted and have at least 1.5mls of blood left before extracting DNA in the HUG medical genetics laboratory. Most weeks we process 200-250 blood samples. In summer 2025 ethics permission was obtained to link to research studies and prioritise samples from research studies. If you have an existing research study or patient registry, or would like to set up a research study, and are interested in banking DNA in a cost efficient manner please contact us at Timothy.frayling@unige.ch. If you wish to collect serum and plasma this may also be possible. For more details see the [How to Use](howtouse.qmd) page.

![The below diagram illustrates how samples are intercepted](../images/BioHUG_2026_outline.png){fig-align="center" width="75%"}
